arts.ttl: Sunderland XI vs Leeds – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Sunderland take on Leeds United in the Championship tonight looking to remain in touch with the automatic places. Régis Le Bris has exceeded expectations at the Stadium of Light to lead Sunderland to fourth in the table. Tonight the Black Cats take on an in-form Leeds side who sit second, seven points above them. arts.ttl: Leeds vs Sunderland – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Leeds United host Sunderland in the Championship this evening as the promotion rivals go head-to-head at Elland Road. Daniel Farke’s side are second the race for promotion and have been in scintillating recent form. Leeds have won seven of their last eight games and have not conceded a goal during that run, with their goal […] The post Leeds vs Sunderland – Predicted lineup and team news first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Sheffield United given triple injury boost ahead of huge Leeds United tie
arts.sum: Sheffield United are set to welcome Gustavo Hamer, Tom Cannon and Kieffer Moore back for the clash with… The post Sheffield United given triple injury boost ahead of huge Leeds United tie appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Leeds v Sunderland: Could Meslier have last laugh tonight?
arts.sum: Leeds United’s clash against Sunderland tonight will prompt horrific flashbacks for Whites fans on Illan Meslier’s howler at the Stadium of Light, but he enters tonight’s game in serious form. This fixture was a hectic one in the reverse, as they shared the spoils in a 2-2 victory that Leeds will wonder how on earth they didn’t win, thanks to a howler in the dying moments from Illan Meslier.

arts.ttl: Jayden Bogle sent Sunderland warning as Leeds United look to land knockout blow
arts.sum: Leeds United know they must not lose against Sunderland to reclaim top spot in the Championship, and will be aiming to extend their winning run to four league games. It means that a win for United over Sunderland will not only down the North East club but also stretch their lead from third place to seven points - a significant gap at this stage of the Championship season.

arts.ttl: Chris Wilder: No pressure on Leeds v Sunderland
arts.sum: Leeds United moved to second on Saturday when Sheffield United secured victory away at Luton Town, with the Whites looking to reclaim top spot and clear a gap to third with victory over Sunderland tonight. Naturally, a promotion rival winning in the days before a big game at Elland Road is going to bring pressure on the result.

arts.ttl: ‘100% quality’: Enzo Le Fée brings poise to Sunderland’s promotion push
arts.sum: Versatile French playmaker adds class and experience to a young side hopeful of returning to the Premier League Enzo Le Fée is the subject of considerable introspection at Roma. The concern at the Stadio Olimpico is that last month’s decision to offload the multitalented French midfielder with more than a hint of Luka Modric about his game, might prove a big mistake. Sunderland certainly exhibit zero sign of buyer’s remorse over an expensive loan deal obligated to turn into a formal £20m transfer should Régis Le Bris’s team return to the Premier League this spring. “Le Fée is 100% quality,” says Gary Bennett, the former Sunderland defender turned BBC Radio Newcastle match analyst. “From what we’ve seen so far, wow. Twenty million will be money very well spent.” Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Chris Wilder may have just given Leeds United summer boost in pursuit of long-term target
arts.sum: Leeds United tried and failed to sign Sheffield United star Gustavo Hamer back in August, but Chris Wilder was unsurprised that interest may still linger in the midfielder. There’s nothing Leeds or any other side can do about wanting to sign Hamer right now, given he’s in the middle of a promotion race and so are we.
